title: eucalyptus spathulata subsp. spathulata
intro: Eucalyptus spathulata subsp. spathulata, also known as the Western Sphagnum-leaved Peppermint Gum, is a species of eucalyptus tree native to Western Australia. The plant is prized for its aromatic leaves, which have been used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Eucalyptus spathulata subsp. spathulata include reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, while also providing relief from insect bites and minor wounds. Eucalyptus spathulata subsp. spathulata can be prepared into various herbal products, including essential oils, teas, and ointments, which can be used topically or inhaled to harness its medicinal properties.
